(A) M: I agree. The temple is really old and it has been worn down. (B) M: I’m sorry, I didn’t catch that. Would you mind speaking more slowly? (C) M: Thank you, you look impressive too. (D) M: I'm glad that you enjoyed your trip today. The temple is very impressive indeed.
label: D

sentence:
M: Would you like to go to right along the lake with me this morning? ,F: I'd like to but I have to go to the city mall with my mother. The new year is coming.
(A) M: Let's have breakfast first and then ride along the lake. See you in a bit! (B) M: I didn't know you went shopping with your mother yesterday. (C) M: I didn’t hear you. Please could you tell me again? (D) M: Have a nice time celebrating the New Year with your mother this morning.
label: C

sentence:
F: Hi Jim, I haven't seen you for several days. Have you moved into your new house already? ,M: Yes, and I'm so excited about it. ,F: Why are you so excited? ,M: I used to live in a small cold room all the year round, but now things have completely changed.
(A) F: You have got married. (B) F: I’m sorry, I didn’t understand. Could you repeat a little louder, please? (C) F: Why are you sad when you move into the new house? Don't you miss the old house? (D) F: You small cold house has been sold. Who buys it?
label: B

sentence:
F: How annoying. This thing breaks down almost once a week. ,M: It needs to be repaired. ,F: It sure does, but we have to walk up 6 flights of stairs at moment. ,M: Well, it's not bad for me to have a walk before work.
(A) F: Excuse me, could you repeat the question? (B) F: Where shall we go by bus? (C) F: The elevator works well. (D) F: The elevator is broken.
label: D

sentence:
M: When does the next bus leave? ,F: You just miss one by 5 minutes. Buses leave every 20 minutes, so you'll have to wait a little.
(A) M: I'll wait here for 25 minutes for the next bus. (B) M: So I have to wait 20 minutes. (C) M: Oh dear, I just missed the bus? So I have to wait 15 minutes more. (D) M: I’m sorry, I didn’t catch that. Would you mind speaking more slowly?
label: D

sentence:
F: Oh dear, I have locked the key in my room again. How I wish I could remember it. ,M: Well, don't worry, These things happen.
(A) F: Yes, People always forget the keys. But I locked them in my room too many times. (B) F: Yes, it's easy to forget the keys, I locked them in my draw. (C) F: Yes, People always make the same mistakes. But this is my first time to lock my keys in the draw. (D) F: I’m sorry, I don’t understand. Could you say it again?
label: A

sentence:
M: Do you take cream and sugar in your coffee? ,F: No, thanks. The doctor told me to eat less sweet food.
(A) M: I'm not surprised that you don't like drinking coffee. (B) M: Excuse me, could you repeat the question? (C) M: You want to buy some sweet food? Let's go shopping then. (D) M: You went to see the doctor? I'm surprised he advised you to eat more sweet food.
label: B

sentence:
F: I'd like to buy some gifts for my dad's birthday this weekend. Any ideas? ,M: There are so many to choose from, such as flowers, ties and so on.
(A) F: Where do you plan to spend your holiday? (B) F: I’m sorry, I didn’t catch that. Would you mind speaking more slowly? (C) F: A tie for my dad is OK. (D) F: I want to hold a birthday party, will you join us?
label: C

sentence:
F: Oh, look, you're so dirty. What happened? ,M: I went to the park to help remove the rubbish and now the park is very clean.
(A) F: Why did you play in the park and look dirty? (B) F: That's very nice of you to remove the rubbish of our garden. (C) F: I am sorry. You spoke so rapidly that I did not catch the meaning of what you said. Could you repeat that? (D) F: Why did you fix the shower?
label: C

sentence:
M: Jane, what is your favorite sport? ,F: Well, I play basketball a lot, but I like tennis even better. How about you?
(A) M: I like playing tennis, but it's a pity to hear that you don't like it. (B) M: I like playing tennis best too, just like you. (C) M: I’m sorry, I don’t understand. Could you say it again? (D) M: I like playing basketball best too, just like you.
label: B

sentence:
M: What did you do with your computer? ,F: Nothing, but it wouldn't work. ,M: I'm afraid we don't have anymore of these models. You can get a refund with your receipt.
(A) F: I am sorry. You spoke so rapidly that I did not catch the meaning of what you said. Could you repeat that? (B) F: Because I'm in the computer lab, so you have these models, right? (C) F: Since I'm in the computer store and you don't have these models, why can't you give me a refund? (D) F: You mean that although I'm in the computer store, you don't have these models, do you?
label: D

sentence:
M: I really have to thank Phi for letting me use this office. ,F: Please don't mention Phi. He's off at a Zurich conference and I'm stuck here with a double patient load.
(A) M: I’m sorry, I didn’t understand. Could you repeat a little louder, please? (B) M: You have more patients to meet. Hold on for several days and Phi will come back from Paris. (C) M: It seems that there are more patients waiting for you. I'll come later. (D) M: Thank you for sharing your office with me.
label: C
